what_it_does: Records a design interpretation (P15) that maps Obsidian features against the 'host not master' principle and concludes Obsidian should be used only as a read-only, frontmatter-reading lens, never as the canonical keeper of typed edges or a write surface.
observations: The item is a ledger block with full frontmatter (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps).
The body frames Obsidian's features along an axis of read-only usefulness versus edge-dependency and write-back risk.
Read-only features (search, Bases, iOS reading surface) are praised as near-free wins that do not touch the canonical fabric.
Edge-dependent features (graph view) and write features (Properties editing, Sync write-back) are rejected because Obsidian cannot model typed addresses and writes reopen two-writer conflict.
The typed graph is declared to remain internal; the desired graph value should come from finishing the system's own walkable graph (design-map A2/A6).
The highest-value item, mobile Obsidian, is gated on two unresolved facts: WSL-vs-Windows-sync infrastructure and title legibility.
It links to a parent noticeboard item via board://item-3480c157.
standouts: Adopt Obsidian as a read-only lens over frontmatter, never as the keeper of edges.
Mobile Obsidian is gated on WSL-vs-Windows-sync infrastructure and title-legibility facts, not on a simple toggle.
The system's own declared-unbuilt walkable graph is the right lens for graph value, not Obsidian's lossy wikilink shadow.
conventions: Follows the ledger frontmatter schema with typed address, source, state, author_session, channel, and part_of link.
Uses markdown body with a title header and bulleted reasoning.
References other ledger items with a typed board:// URI.
Employs project-specific terminology such as 'host not master,' 'typed graph,' 'edges,' and 'design-map A2/A6'.
concerns: The conclusion relies on two unresolved infrastructure/UX facts that are named but not explained or resolved in the file.
The 'axes' reading is presented as a clean result without showing the underlying evidence or comparison matrix.
It mixes a strong recommendation with dependency caveats without separating decision from preconditions.
notes: A concise design ruling that Obsidian should remain a read-only frontmatter viewer, with mobile adoption blocked by two unresolved infrastructure and legibility facts.
questions: What exactly is the 'WSL-vs-Windows-sync infra fact' and how is it being evaluated?
What does the 'title-legibility fact' refer to in practice?
What is the content of the parent item board://item-3480c157?
Which Obsidian features were compared or tested to produce the axes reading?
What is the timeline or owner for finishing the system's own walkable graph?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to read off the axes the shape of 'host not master'; the file does exactly that by categorizing Obsidian features and deriving a read-only recommendation, so purpose matches actual.
apparent_themes: host not master / canonical source of truth stays internal
typed graph versus generic wikilink graph
frontmatter as a portable read interface
intention_signals: It is trying to settle a tool-adoption debate by reframing Obsidian as a passive viewing layer rather than a competing editor or graph database, and to redirect graph-value effort toward completing the system's own structured graph.
novelty: It rejects Obsidian's native graph and write features not on general quality grounds but because they conflict with a typed-address edge model and two-writer risk — a principle-first tool evaluation rather than a feature checklist.
contribution: A principle-bound evaluation frame for integrating external tools: classify features by read-only versus edge-dependent versus write-back, surface infrastructure and legibility blockers explicitly, and keep the canonical typed graph internal.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design item that interprets the 'host not master' principle for Obsidian integration, concluding Obsidian should be read-only over frontmatter while edge modeling and writes remain in the canonical system.
intention_for_embedding: To record a decision and its caveats about how Obsidian may be used as a passive lens without becoming a second writer or graph source.
